cells.xml_text_content: North East Yorkshire and the Humber
cells.pdf_text_content: North East Yorkshire and the Humber

cells.xml_text_content: Source: Decision Making and Appeals System (DMAS), via Stat-Xplore
cells.pdf_text_content: Source: Decision Making and Appeals System (DMAS), via Stat-Xplore

cells.xml_text_content: 1    Cells in this table have been randomly adjusted to avoid the release of confidential data.  Due to adjustments totals may not be the sum of the individual cells.
cells.pdf_text_content: 1    Cells in this table have been randomly adjusted to avoid the release of confidential data.  Due to adjustments totals may not be the sum of the individual cells.

cells.xml_text_content: 2    The current Jobcentre Plus structure, implemented on 5 April 2011, reduced the JobCentre Plus Geography hierarchy from 11 regions and 48 districts to 7 groups and 37 districts.
cells.pdf_text_content: 2    The current Jobcentre Plus structure, implemented on 5 April 2011, reduced the JobCentre Plus Geography hierarchy from 11 regions and 48 districts to 7 groups and 37 districts.

cells.xml_text_content: 3    A non-adverse decision is a decision found in favour of the claimant, i.e. a sanction or disallowance is not applied.
cells.pdf_text_content: 3    A non-adverse decision is a decision found in favour of the claimant, i.e. a sanction or disallowance is not applied.

cells.xml_text_content: 4    An adverse decision is a decision found against the claimant, i.e. a sanction or disallowance is applied.
cells.pdf_text_content: 4    An adverse decision is a decision found against the claimant, i.e. a sanction or disallowance is applied.

cells.xml_text_content: 5     A cancelled decision can occur in specific circumstances e.g. the claimant stops claiming before they actually committed the sanctionable failure.
cells.pdf_text_content: 5     A cancelled decision can occur in specific circumstances e.g. the claimant stops claiming before they actually committed the sanctionable failure.
